title: Pityriasis rosea
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Pityriasis rosea is a common rash that often affects young adults.
The rash can begin with one larger patch followed by smaller oval scaly spots on the trunk or limbs.
Symptoms may include itching and can resolve over weeks.
Pregnancy, mucosal lesions, medication reaction concern, systemic illness, or diagnostic uncertainty require separate handling.
